input.background: The thickness of a steel sheet follows a uniform distribution with values between 150 and 200 millimeters. An experiment is performed repeatedly: randomly sampling 11 steel sheets from the production line and recording the thickness, $x$, of each. Two sample statistics, the sample mean ($\bar{x}$) and the median ($m$), are calculated. The sample mean is defined as $\bar{x}=\frac{\Sigma x}{11}$, and the median is defined as the sixth sample measurement when the 11 thicknesses are arranged in ascending order. The goal is to approximate the sampling distributions of $\bar{x}$ and $m$.
input.question: What are the sample mean ($\bar{x}$) and median ($m$) for the first computer-generated sample?

output.answer:
The first computer-generated sample (arranged in ascending order) contains the following thickness measurements: $151, 157, 162, 169, 171, 173, 181, 182, 187, 188, 193$ millimeters. The sample mean and median for this sample are:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \bar{x}=\frac{151+157+\cdots+193}{11}=174.0 \\
& m=\text{ Sixth ordered measurement }=173
\end{aligned}
$$
